text:
The small town pie-maker struggled alone with her craft. She baked little pies at night, dreaming of flavors that would leap off the plate. It was just her and the moonlight. One evening, she dreamed she won a contest. When she woke up, she looked out her window at the silver moon. She pulled out her apron—complete with moon-patterned buttons—got to work, and made herself a prize-winning blueberry pie.

The contest was big news in the town, and everyone wanted a piece of that pie. The townsfolk gathered, eyes bright on the creamy blueberry. They bit into the pie—I'll never forget that crunch. They smiled too—a moonlit smile.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
